name: Cancho de la Cabeza por la Senda del Genaro – circular desde Patones de Abajo
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Patones are set within the Sierra Norte region of Madrid, characterized by dramatic slate mountains, lush forests, and the picturesque Jarama Valley. The area features the expansive El Atazar Reservoir, a focal point for many routes, and distinct "black architecture" villages. This diverse landscape offers varied terrain, from technical descents to challenging climbs, making it a notable destination for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Patones The most popular mountain bike trail is GR 300 - Genaro Trail, a 40.5 miles (65.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 34 minutes to complete. This challenging loop offers varied terrain and scenic vistas around the El Atazar reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cancho de la Cabeza por la Senda del Genaro – circular desde Patones de Abajo, a moderate 14.9 miles (24.0 km) path. This route provides panoramic views from Cancho de la Cabeza overlooking the El Atazar reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Cancho de la Cabeza and Senda del Genaro Loop from Patones, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through the rugged terrain around Cancho de la Cabeza, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Patones is defined by slate mountains, the Jarama Valley, and the El Atazar Reservoir. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Patones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Patones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I expect in Patones?
A: Patones offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from moderate to difficult, set amidst dramatic slate mountains, lush forests, and the picturesque Jarama Valley. You'll find routes with varied terrain, including technical descents, challenging climbs, and segments through unique "black architecture" villages. The area is particularly known for its Enduro MTB routes, featuring rocky sections, flowy forest paths, and technical steps over slate rocks.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Patones, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Patones. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 1 easy trail, 5 moderate trails, and 8 difficult trails, ensuring options for most riders.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Patones?
A: Yes, Patones features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961261036/gr-300-genaro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 300 - Genaro Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 40.5-mile loop around the El Atazar reservoir. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961265925/cancho-de-la-cabeza-por-la-senda-del-genaro-circular-desde-patones-de-abajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza por la Senda del Genaro – circular desde Patones de Abajo&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: What are the best trails for experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging routes in Patones. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961261036/gr-300-genaro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 300 - Genaro Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 40.5-mile loop with significant elevation changes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350471053/patones-cancho-de-la-cabeza-and-carcavas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Patones, Cancho de la Cabeza, and Cárcavas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 21.9 miles with over 1,100 meters of ascent, known for its rugged terrain and technical sections.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails in Patones?
A: Many trails offer stunning views of the region's natural landmarks. You can ride past the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the largest in Madrid. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt; provides breathtaking panoramic views of the reservoir and the distant Peña de la Cabra. You might also spot griffon vultures and peregrine falcons overhead in the river valleys.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can explore Patones de Arriba, a beautifully preserved village known for its unique "black architecture" made from local slate. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23749500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Parra Weir – Pontón de la Oliva Dam loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historical Pontón de la Oliva dam, a site related to the region's industrial heritage. The Dehesa de la Oliva also hosts an archaeological site and the Romanesque-Mudéjar hermitage Virgen de la Oliva.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Patones?
A: The mountain bike trails in Patones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the scenic vistas around the El Atazar reservoir, and the challenging nature of the routes, especially those with technical descents and significant elevation changes.

Q: Is Patones a good destination for mountain biking with family or children?
A: While Patones is known for its challenging and technical trails, there is 1 easy trail among the 14 available routes. For families with younger or less experienced riders, it's advisable to carefully review the difficulty grades and elevation profiles of specific routes to ensure they are suitable. The ecological path of El Barranco, connecting Patones de Abajo and Patones de Arriba, offers a scenic and less strenuous option for walking or gentle cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Patones?
A: The Sierra Norte region of Madrid, where Patones is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures and less extreme weather, making for more comfortable rides. Summer can be very hot, especially at midday, while winter might bring colder temperatures and occasional precipitation, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in Patones?
A: Yes, the villages of Patones de Abajo and Patones de Arriba offer amenities such as cafes and pubs where you can refuel and relax after your ride. Patones de Arriba, with its charming slate architecture, is a particularly pleasant place to stop for refreshments.

Q: Are there any specific trails that offer views of the El Atazar Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961261036/gr-300-genaro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 300 - Genaro Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging loop that circles the entire El Atazar Reservoir, providing continuous scenic vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961265925/cancho-de-la-cabeza-por-la-senda-del-genaro-circular-desde-patones-de-abajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza por la Senda del Genaro – circular desde Patones de Abajo&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the reservoir from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This peak is one of the most visited in the northern mountains due to its accessibility and its spectacular 360º views. From here you can see: To the north the Atazar reservoir and the Sierra de Ayllón, to the west the Sierra de La Cabrera and, on a clear day, the Peñalara peak, which is the highest in Madrid, to the south you can see the entire plain of Madrid and part of its skyline.

One of the funniest sections of the Sierra de Norte de Madrid. The whole area is full of perfect mountain bike trails suitable for cyclists new to trail riding, some steep and technical, others like this one full of fun in incredible scenery.

Patones de Arriba is a municipality in the Sierra Norte region of Madrid. Its historic center was declared a site of cultural interest for being one of the best preserved black towns. This type of architecture is characterized by the use of constructive elements in dark tones, such as slate.
